title: Animation & State Machines
description: Master Animation Blueprints, skeletal meshes, blend spaces, and animation state transition rules.
learningObjectives: Differentiate the AnimGraph (pose generation) from the EventGraph (data gathering)
Configure 1D and 2D Blend Spaces to smooth character motion transitions
Design locomotion State Machines managing idle, walking, and jumping pose states

sections.title: Animation State Dispatching
sections.content:
Animation Blueprints control skeletal mesh transformations. The class divides responsibilities into:

1. **EventGraph**: Updates variables (like character speed or falling states) by querying the possessed pawn.
2. **AnimGraph**: Evaluation pipeline generating skeletal frame poses based on variables (using Blend Spaces to merge walking/running speeds, and State Machines to route transition rules).

sections.title: Jumping Transition Rule
sections.content: In the state machine graph, transitioning from Locomotion to JumpStart is governed by a transition rule checking a boolean variable: `IsInAir == true` (updated via the EventGraph query tick).
